core_mechanism: Exact, nonperturbative solution of two linearly (bilinearly) coupled oscillatory modes — one heavy, one light — via hypergeometric Green's functions, in which the heavy mode's intrinsic frequency imprints log-periodic, non-analytic oscillations on the light mode's correlations (mode-mixing 'spectroscopy' resolved to all orders in the coupling rather than perturbatively)
brief_reason: The abstract treats the constant-turn quadratic mixing between a light curvature mode and a massive field nonperturbatively with exact hypergeometric propagators, then uses a Mellin–Barnes representation to isolate non-analytic late-time branches whose logarithmic-oscillation frequency encodes the heavy mass while amplitude and phase keep the full mixing dependence.
